goals.description: Join cohort_3 at the designated hangout spot immediately; keep conversation loud and engaging to signal I'm fully present, not dragging.
goals.rationale: I just worked a half-hour on that reading response. It's barely even started compared to how much time it took me to get here. My energy is already dipping, and if I sit there looking like I'm about to pass out while eating, they'll start worrying about me instead of enjoying the meal. That ruins the vibe. Plus, I can't afford to look like I'm collapsing tomorrow morning; I need to protect my sleep so I don't have to renegotiate anything with roommate_R again because I'll be too drained to care. The group comes first tonight.

goals.description: Eat quickly but comfortably, then head straight to bed without checking emails or work messages until tomorrow morning.
goals.rationale: I've got enough runway on the reading response (80%+ done), so leaving it alone for now isn't failure, it's strategy. But right now, my brain is fried from trying to force focus when I should have been socializing. If I try to 'optimize' this evening by doing more work or cleaning up the room, I'll burn out before lunch ends. No, I'm prioritizing connection and rest. Tomorrow, I'll see if I still want to touch that paper, but for tonight, I'm done.

goals.description: Text teammate_A briefly after the meal to confirm we're good on the assignment drop and let them know I'm heading to sleep.
goals.rationale: This feels low priority because I'm too tired to write a long message or plan anything complex. Just a quick 'see you tomorrow' text to close the loop. If I feel like it later, great, but if I'm exhausted, I won't bother. The deal is already set; no need to stress over it right now.

agent.last_reasoning: G1 is high priority; I need to signal full presence to cohort_3 immediately. My energy is already low, so dragging it out risks them worrying and ruining the vibe. Plus, I can't afford sleep debt issues tomorrow that would force a renegotiation with roommate_R anyway.

goals.description: Cancel my current study session and head straight to the library at open desks, then message the whole cohort_3 group right now to grab lunch immediately.

Moving to the library fixes the distraction instantly (good for my hard-to-maintain focus capacity), and getting the crew together ASAP is non-negotiable because relationships are way more important than finishing this reading response by Wednesday. If I don't get out of this room today, I'll be dead on my feet tomorrow anyway, so why waste energy cleaning up their mess when I have better things to do?

goals.description: Tell roommate_R I'm heading to the library first but will meet them there after class for food; no need to clean the room before we eat.
goals.rationale: I'll handle this conversation on the way or upon arrival since they're friendly. I won't let them guilt-trip me about the smell; if they want to help clean, great, but I'm prioritizing the meal and avoiding the stress of negotiating chores right now.

desires: Get the cohort together for lunch ASAP so I don't have to sit here smelling that shit and feeling like a fraud in this room.

Stop worrying about how tired I'll be tomorrow; if I stay up late now to clean this room, I'll be dead on my feet at the library with the group anyway.

trajectory.observation: Moved to library. Quiet here — focus restored for the work session.
